mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier discounting and a lower hurdle rate for equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines but subsequent 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of such cases, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays near the effective lower bound, real-rate relief and easier liquidity conditions support equity multiples, producing positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not precede positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or if the sign of the effect reverses when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend marks labor-market deterioration and future earnings downgrades, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE surge episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the 3-year rolling Rank IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth in RSAFS indicates resilient final demand, supporting revenue expectations and improving S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend RSAFS growth fails to precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the rolling 2-year information coefficient remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in INDPRO and HOUST reflects improving production and construction activity, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and support positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise month over month for 2 consecutive releases
failure_condition: Reject if the joint up-move in INDPRO and HOUST does not outperform either series alone for predicting 20-day SPX returns, or if triggered instances produce negative returns in a majority of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A falling PAYEMS print after prior gains signals payroll slowdown and weaker income growth, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month over month after a 3-month average above the prior 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if payroll declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the signal's short-horizon Rank IC is not negative over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 slows while RSAFS remains strong, markets should interpret the mix as temporary inventory and composition support rather than broad recession, favoring positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates from the prior quarter while RSAFS remains above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the mixed GDP-slowdown/retail-strength regime is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term is not stronger than either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, an elevated FEDFUNDS rate transmits through tighter financial conditions and multiple compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month median and CPIAUCSL inflation remains above target trend
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate tightening episodes do not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the effect disappears after conditioning on CPIAUCSL.
